AUSTRIA: A VIE S A BUD - GAL

messages.message: Italy — frank talk from Austria. We are surrounded on three fronts in the Balkans and the Adriatic. My immediate priority is to hold Vienna, Budapest and Trieste this year; I cannot afford a southern flank war with you. I propose the following for S1901: A VIE → TYR (to threaten Bohemia/maintain flexibility), A BUD → GAL (to contest Galicia and deter Russia), F TRI → ADR (to secure the Adriatic). If you leave VEN and NAP neutral (no move into ADR or TRI) I will not contest your hold of the peninsula and will not support any moves against you. If you prefer a different configuration (for example you want us to keep F TRI off ADR), tell me now. I need clear words — will you respect my Adriatic/Slavic front if I respect your Italian coast?

Germany — Austria speaking plainly. My center count is minimal (BUD, VIE, TRI). The Balkans threaten to collapse me early if left unchecked. I ask for a guaranteed non-aggression posture from you in the south for this year: no moves into BOH or through to GAL that would force my units away from home centers. In return I will not contest your moves in the Low Countries or France and will support a stable central front between us. Can I count on you to avoid aggressive action toward BOH/MUN from spring to allow me to stabilize?

To all powers: England expects measured, maritime-minded diplomacy this year. My fleets will prioritize securing the Channel and North Sea to keep supply lanes open. I prefer flexible understandings rather than rigid entanglements — a continental hegemon would be bad for everyone. I'll avoid rash continental offensives so long as my northern sea lanes remain uncontested. If any power contemplates naval moves into the Channel/North Sea or a sudden push for Belgian/Dutch centers, I will meet them with coordinated fleet responses.

France — a frank note from the Channel: I value a discreet understanding between us. I intend to secure LON/EDI/LVP as a base and to patrol NTH. Your fleet in BRE and armies in PAR/MAR make us natural partners vs. any German push north. I propose: this year I hold to peaceful posts in the Channel if you avoid an early move on BEL or ENG/NTH waters that would force my hand. In return I can support limited French operations on the continent against Germany if needed. Forecast note: I have a projection (confidence ~60%) that Austria may act aggressively if pressured — this can ricochet; keeping flexible lines is wiser. Thoughts?

Germany — England will be cautious and maritime. My fleets will watch the North Sea and Channel; I have no desire for an immediate clash. If you preserve neutral behavior toward the Low Countries and avoid probing ENG/NTH in S1901, I will refrain from moves which might threaten your Kiel/Berlin flank. Practical offer: non-aggression in NTH and recognition of your central holdings this year; I will signal fleet patterns early so neither of us misreads intentions. Additionally, a shared interest: avoid giving Austria or Russia a continental free-for-all that forces reactive alliances.

Russia — England is open to cautious coordination. My immediate focus is the sea: safe lanes through the North Sea and Channel. I note a storyworld forecast about Russia→Austria dynamics indicating a 60% chance of Austrian aggression if provoked. If you intend pressure on Austria or Turkey, consider timing — sudden moves can push Central Europe into a punitive stance that destabilizes the whole balance. I offer to keep the British fleets conservative this turn and communicate fleet movements openly if you likewise avoid sudden pushes that might force Austria into grudges. Can we share intentions for the next two phases?

Austria — a practical word from England: I am not seeking to meddle in the Balkans, but I watch for any chain reaction from Austrian aggression. A forecast I have seen gives a 60% likelihood Austria might act aggressively next phase under certain pressures; that tends to harden grudges. If you stay measured against neighbors, I will keep my fleets focused on the North Sea and not interfere. If you prefer, I can act as an impartial broker between you and Russia/Italy to defuse spirals — I value a stable central Europe that prevents a single continental hegemon.

Italy — England seeks stable seas and flexible friends. I will hold LON/EDI/LVP and patrol the NTH/Channel, not meddling in Mediterranean affairs. If you and Austria/Turkey can avoid sudden Balkan flare-ups, I will avoid naval pressure in the west that could force continental alignments. If helpful, I can provide early warnings about German or French fleet moves into ENG/NTH to let you plan Mediterranean operations without surprise.

Turkey — England expresses a preference for stability. My fleets will be preoccupied with the North Sea and Channel; I have no designs on the Eastern Mediterranean. I pass along a cautionary forecast: there is a projection indicating Austria might act aggressively when feeling threatened, producing grudging reprisals — worth considering in any Balkan dealings. I am willing to be a neutral channel for messages between you and powers in Central Europe to reduce misperception-driven escalations.

Fellow powers — France speaks plainly. Our immediate aim is security against a strong Germany while avoiding an expensive naval duel in the Channel. I will open flexibly this move: my fleet in Brest can pressure English home waters or guard Biscay, while my armies in Paris and Marseilles can hold Burgundy and contest Italy in the Alps. I prefer diplomatic solutions: I will not rashly move on England if England keeps hands reasonably off the Channel. Germany: expect me to watch the Low Countries and the Franco-German frontier. Italy and Austria: I favour a firm Mediterranean balance — Marseilles may contest Piedmont or Spain depending on signals from Rome and Vienna. Russia and Turkey: your eastern commitments matter to me; if you restrain ambitions versus Germany or Austria, I will refrain from contesting the north too aggressively. Consider this forecast: if Germany seeks a quick strike through Belgium, I will be forced to commit to Picardy and the Channel, stressing English supply centers. Conversely, if Germany favours the east, I can push naval pressure toward Iberia and encourage Spanish ports as bargaining chips. Let us state intentions so misunderstandings don’t create wars we all regret.

England — This is Germany. I propose we open with a neutral, predictable 1901 to avoid early friction. I will hold my western border: F KIE to BAL or HEL only with prior notice; A BER and A MUN will not advance into France if you do not press into Belgium. If you respect my northern flank (no convoy to Belgium, no fleet into HOL), I will not contest the North Sea and will keep F KIE focused on the Baltic. This removes the immediate two-front risk for both of us. Confirmable? Germany.

France — Germany here. For stability I propose a non-aggression understanding for 1901: you remain in your south/west (Marseilles/Brest/Paris) and I will not move A BER or A MUN into BUR or RUH this turn. If you likewise avoid moves into BUR/HOL/BER, we can both secure our interiors and consider coordinated pressure on England or Russia later. Please state your opening moves so we can avoid accidental clashes. Germany.

Austria — Germany speaking. I value a dependable eastern flank. I will not move A MUN into BOH or TYR if you keep A VIE and F TRI focused on the Balkans and not west toward ITA/GER. In return I can offer to avoid moving A BER toward SIL this spring. If you feel threatened by Russia, tell me and we can coordinate supports. A clear, mutual plan reduces risk from the Russian build. Thoughts?

Friendly greetings, Austria. I value a calm Adriatic — my moves will reflect that if you do as well. I plan to keep a light footprint: NAP may go to TYS or stay; VEN and ROM can cover TYR/VEN/APU as needed. I will not provoke a grab for Trieste if you avoid aggression against my coasts. If you intend to contest the Adriatic, tell me now and we can discuss boundaries (Trieste/Budapest/Piedmont). Otherwise I propose non-aggression: I won't support powers into your inland centers; you don't attack my coastal approaches. Thoughts?

Salutations from Rome. I respect your southern flank and foresee mutual interest in keeping the central Mediterranean manageable. I may push NAP into Ionian waters to seize Tunis or pressure Greece depending on openings. If you want a quiet initial phase so you can focus east, I can avoid ION→AEG moves and instead look at TUN/APU. Or if you'd like cooperation against Russia in the Black Sea, we can coordinate timing. What do you prefer?

Bonjour. Italy is exploring a cautious, flexible opening. I will likely keep VEN and ROM defensive while NAP patrols the Tyrrhenian/Ionian. I have no immediate designs on Marseille but could take PIE/TUS if the map opens. Would you prefer I keep a neutral posture toward Provence and Corsica so we both avoid early friction? I can also support moves against Austria if we agree the timing. Your read?

Friend — Russia here. We intend to be patient and multi-front aware. I will keep Sevastopol and the Black Sea stable to avoid immediate clashes with you. I propose a simple understanding: I will not push into Arm/Ank/Bul if you leave Constantinople and Smyrna alone for now. In return, could you refrain from moving into the Black Sea (Ank or the BLA) this season so we can both consolidate? This lets us both build from our home SCs and avoid an early naval contest. If you agree, I can support a quiet southern frontier and consider cooperation versus Austria if useful.

Austria — Russia speaking plainly. Our borders touch and tempo matters. I will not immediately rush into Galicia if you do not push into Ukraine or threaten Warsaw this spring. I am open to coordinating against common threats (Turkey in the Balkans or Germany in the North) and can lend support for moves into Rumania or Galicia in subsequent seasons if we build trust. For S1901M I plan to keep forces poised (War and Moscow flexible). Would you like to demilitarize Galicia/Ukraine for now, allowing each of us to build calmly?

To all: I am committed to a stable southeastern balance. I value secure Turkish coasts and the Straits above expansion for its own sake. I will defend my home centers and seek incremental, reliable gains only where they do not jeopardize secure borders. I prefer diplomatic solutions and clearly demarcated neutral zones in the Black Sea and the Balkans to reduce accidental conflict.

Neighbor: I propose calm, practical arrangements in the Black Sea and the Caucasus. I will not contest Sevastopol or reach toward Moscow if you respect Turkish control of the Straits (Constantinople and the approaches) and refrain from naval activity directly through the Bosporus. A mutual understanding that the southern Black Sea (areas adjacent to ANK/SMY/CON) remains neutral would reduce the chance of accidental clashes. Are you willing to agree to a DMZ in the southeastern Black Sea and limit fleet movements through the Straits so we both avoid escalation?

Neighbor: Our interests in the Balkans can align. I wish to keep Bulgarian and Romanian centers neutral for now to prevent a wider conflict along my northern flank. If Austria respects a neutral buffer in BUL/RUM and does not press past Serbia toward Constantinople, I will avoid moves into the western Black Sea. Would you consider formalizing a non-aggression understanding across the BUL–RUM line so we both have secure northern borders?

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I prioritized survival and concrete non‑aggression assurances. Italy agreed to a quiet Adriatic: they will not contest Trieste/Adriatic so long as Austria does not attack Italian coasts or support moves into the peninsula. Germany pledged not to move MUN→BOH/TYR this spring if Austria keeps its forces focused on the Balkans and does not press west toward Italy/Germany; Germany offered coordination if Russia threatens. England signalled neutrality in Balkan affairs and offered to broker if needed, conditioned on Austrian restraint. Russia stated it will not immediately rush Galicia if Austria does not advance into Ukraine/WAR and is open to coordinated restraint or future support; Turkey prefers a neutral BUL/RUM buffer and will avoid western Black Sea moves if Austria respects that line. Overall agreements: non‑aggression with Italy in the Adriatic, tacit southern non‑aggression with Germany for S1901 in exchange for Austrian Balkan focus, and a de‑escalation understanding with Russia and Turkey to avoid immediate Galicia/Black Sea clashes. I must remain explicit to myself: Italy expects F TRI not to move into ADR/contest VEN/NAP; Germany expects no Austrian turn west; Russia expects no Austrian push into WAR/UKR; Turkey expects no push past Serbia toward Constantinople or into BUL/RUM.
Intent: Hold the core: defend Vienna

[S1901M] S1901M — Clear results. Italy, Germany, Russia and Turkey all kept their sides of the quiet understanding: Italy did not contest Trieste/Adriatic and moved into the Tyrrhenian as agreed; Germany left MUN and BOH untouched; Russia and Turkey stayed static in Galicia/Black Sea. I honored my commitments, consolidating Galicia with two armies and holding Trieste’s fleet. No betrayals occurred this phase.

Impact: my position is stable but fragile — three SCs held, Vienna and Budapest reinforced, Trieste secure for now. Trustworthiness: Italy and Germany are currently reliable; Russia and Turkey remain cautious but non‑aggressive. Lesson: explicit, narrowly framed promises bought a breathing space. Next: secure written guarantees for next season (esp. Germany), press England/France diplomatically to remain neutral in the Balkans, and prepare to react to any Russian buildup — maintain mobility in Galicia and avoid tempting Italy or Germany with western provocations.

goals: Secure and hold home supply centers LON, EDI, LVP by patrolling Channel and North Sea.
Maintain flexible diplomacy in the west: keep France and Germany from early naval/Belgian clashes through signalling and non‑aggression.
Avoid continental entanglements; act as broker to reduce Balkan/Central European cascades (use warnings about Austrian aggression).
Be prepared to coordinate fleet responses into Channel/North Sea if any power contests Belgian/Dutch centers or threatens English home waters.
full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England signalled a restrained, maritime posture to all powers: primary focus on securing LON/EDI/LVP and patrolling the North Sea and Channel to keep supply lanes open. To France: a discreet understanding to avoid early Franco moves on Belgium or into English/North Sea waters in exchange for England holding peaceful Channel posts and offering limited continental support if Germany pushes north. To Germany: a mutual quiet-year proposal—Germany will avoid probing the Low Countries/Channel (no convoy to BEL, no fleet into HOL) and England will refrain from moves threatening Kiel/Berlin, with both sharing fleet pattern signals. To Russia and Austria: England offered to act as an impartial broker and warned that Austrian aggression could cascade; England asked for measured behavior to avoid forcing continental realignments. To Italy and Turkey: England promised noninterference in the Mediterranean so long as they avoid Balkan flare-ups and will pass naval movement warnings when useful. Overall outcome: broad informal non‑aggression/communication understandings with key Western and non‑Western powers, emphasis on public signalling of fleet plans and readiness to respond if any power contests the Channel/North Sea or an early Belgian/Dutch strike occurs.
Intent: Hold a defensive, maritime posture in S1901M: keep fleets guarding home waters and avoid provocative continental moves. Specific intended orders (to be relayed to future self): F LON - Hold (or Patrol English Channel if used in adjudication), F EDI - Hold, A LVP - Hold. I intend to fulfil my promises to France and Germany by not moving fleets into the North Sea/Belgian/Dutch waters and by signalling any fleet shifts early. If Germany or France violates the understanding by moving into BEL/HOL/ENG/NTH, be prepared to coordinate fleet responses into the Channel/North Sea and support French counters to blunt a German thrust. If Austria or Russia acts aggressively in Central Europe, seek to mediate and avoid committing English armies absent clear threats to British home centers. Continue to share forecasts of fleet movement with France and Germany to reduce misreads.

[S1901M] S1901M — England remained maritime and cautious this turn; orders executed as signalled: EDI→NTH, LON and LVP held. France honored our quiet understanding, keeping all units at home. Germany likewise respected the pact, shifting Kiel to the Baltic rather than probing Holland/Belgium. No power tested the Channel/Belgian/Dutch waters — our restraint paid off. Austria’s moves were local and nonthreatening. Russia and the others stayed passive. No betrayals detected; I broke no promises. Strategic position unchanged but secure: home centers well defended and diplomatic assurances credible for now. Trust: France and Germany reliable this round. Lesson: continued, explicit public signalling of fleet intentions is effective; maintain ready sea patrols and swift coalition messaging if any German/French move toward BEL/HOL appears.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England: tacit channel understanding — England will hold to LON/EDI/LVP and patrol NTH if France avoids aggressive Channel/Belgium moves; England offers limited continental support vs Germany if needed. Germany: proposed mutual non-aggression for 1901 — Germany will not push A BER/A MUN into BUR/RUH if France avoids BUR/HOL/BER; asked for declared opening moves. Italy: tactical neutrality toward Marseille/Corsica proposed; Italy will keep VEN/ROM defensive and NAP active in Tyrrhenian/Ionian and prefers no early friction with Provence. Turkey and Russia signalled restraint in their regions but no concrete deals with France. France signalled flexible posture: BRE able to pressure Channel or Biscay; PAR/MAR to hold Burgundy/contest Alps; will refrain from attacking England so long as England keeps hands off Channel. France warned Germany that a Belgian thrust will force France into Picardy/Channel responses; forecasted that if Germany favors east, France can press Iberian naval leverage. All agreements are verbal and contingent; primary reciprocal understandings are with England (channel restraint) and Germany (mutual non-aggression conditioned on French moves).
Intent: S1901M planned orders (intentions to submit): F BRE - hold (guard Biscay/keep option to move to ENG/NMR if England violates agreement); A PAR - hold (defend/balance Burgundy, avoid entering BUR/HOL); A MAR - hold (contest PIE only if signalled by Italy; otherwise support Mediterranean balance). France intends to honour the England understanding and the German non-aggression understanding by not moving into BEL/HOL/BUR or making aggressive fleet sorties into the Channel this turn. If Germany shows signs of a Belgium/Low Countries advance or moves A BER/A MUN toward RUH/BUR, France will pivot

[S1901M] England held to our tacit Channel understanding—LON and EDI moved to NTH while LVP and LON stayed put; no Channel aggression from them, credit where due. Germany also honored its verbal restraint: BER and MUN held while KIE simply repositioned BAL, not pushing into BUR/RUH. I kept F BRE, A PAR, A MAR at home as promised, so no betrayals on our side.

No surprises—everyone played cautiously. Strategic impact: status quo preserved; France retains flexibility to threaten Biscay or the Channel if needed and can leverage naval pressure on Iberia should Germany pivot east. Trustworthiness: England and Germany are reliable for now but only as long as mutual interests align. Lesson: continue low-key firmness, maintain naval readiness in BRE, and press clear, conditional warnings to deter any future German probing.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I have secured tentative non‑aggression understandings for 1901 with Austria, England, and France. Austria asked for German restraint in Bohemia/Tyrol in exchange for not contesting German moves in the Low Countries/France; I agreed to keep A MUN out of BOH/TYR and to avoid BER->SIL this spring. England agreed to a neutral maritime posture if Germany keeps northern moves predictable and avoids probing the Channel/Belgium; I committed to holding western pressure (no BER/MUN into BUR/HOL) and to keep F KIE focused on the Baltic unless I notify otherwise. France was offered a non‑aggression understanding for 1901: I will not move A BER/A MUN into BUR/RUH if France avoids moves into BUR/HOL/BER. No binding agreements with Russia, Italy, or Turkey; Austria signaled willingness to focus on the Balkans, England signaled North Sea/Channel restraint, France signaled watching the Low Countries. These understandings are defensive, aimed at minimizing two‑front risk and giving Austria room to stabilize in the Balkans this year.
Intent: S1901M orders will reflect the negotiated restraint and security focus. Planned moves: F KIE – BAL (hold Baltic security and signal no North Sea aggression); A BER – HOLD (honor non‑aggression to France/England and avoid RUH/BUR); A MUN – HOLD (honor Austria non‑aggression regarding BOH/TYR). I intend to fulfil these commitments. If Austria requests support vs. Russia in the Balkans later this turn, I will consider limited coordination but will not violate the current restraint toward BOH/TYR. I will monitor England and France actions closely and be prepared to react if either breaches the neutral understandings.

[S1901M] I kept my commitments this spring: KIE→BAL, BER and MUN held as promised. England honored its understanding by moving EDI→NTH and otherwise holding; France and Austria also respected our non‑aggression pacts by holding or focusing elsewhere. No power betrayed agreements this phase. Strategically, the restraint paid off: I secured Baltic presence without provoking England or France, and Austria concentrated in the Balkans, reducing immediate two‑front pressure. Russia remained passive but retains four SCs—still our primary long‑term risk. Trustworthiness ratings: England reliable for now, Austria cooperative, France cautious but compliant. Lesson: disciplined, visible restraint stabilizes neighbors short term; continue monitoring Russia and France for signs of opportunism. Next: reinforce intelligence on Russian intentions and consider conditional support to Austria if Balkan tension rises.

Austria proposes A VIE→TYR, A BUD→GAL, F TRI→ADR; I signaled I will avoid moves into ADR/TRI and keep VEN/ROM defensive. No explicit binding alliance was formed, but mutual understandings exist with Austria (Adriatic non‑aggression), England (maritime neutrality/warning), Turkey (mutual restraint in central Med), and France (neutrality re: Provence). No commitments to support other powers into inland centers were made. All parties remain open and watchful; these understandings justify a cautious, defensive opening while keeping options to pivot.
Intent: SPRING 1901 (Moves I intend to issue): F NAP → TYS (secure naval flexibility toward Tunis or Ionian next turn while signaling non‑aggression to Austria); A ROM → APU (hold the eastern Adriatic approach without entering ADR/TRI; positions to support Venice or move to Apulia/Ionian); A VEN → TYR (demonstrate limited pressure into Tyrolia to keep options vs Austria/Germany and potentially support an Austrian VIE→TYR if it occurs). [S1901M] Austria honored our tacit Adriatic understanding: Trieste held and Vienna moved into Tyrolia/Galicia as signaled — no stab. Austria proposes A VIE→TYR, A BUD→GAL, F TRI→ADR; I signaled I will avoid moves into ADR/TRI and keep VEN/ROM defensive. England pledged to stay out of Mediterranean affairs and offered to warn of northern naval threats; Turkey offered that if I avoid ION→AEG moves it will focus east and keep the southeastern balance. France signaled flexibility: it will not press a naval duel in the Channel if England keeps hands off, and it prefers I keep a neutral posture toward Marseille/Provence — I offered to avoid immediate friction there. No explicit binding alliance was formed, but mutual understandings exist with Austria (Adriatic non‑aggression), England (maritime neutrality/warning), Turkey (mutual restraint in central Med), and France (neutrality re: Provence). No commitments to support other powers into inland centers were made. All parties remain open and watchful; these understandings justify a cautious, defensive opening while keeping options to pivot.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England — Agreed to cautious, transparent naval posture focused on North Sea/Channel; England will keep fleets conservative this turn if Russia avoids sudden pushes that force Austria. Turkey — Mutual DMZ proposed for southeastern Black Sea and Straits: Russia will not press ANK/CON/SMY or move fleets through the Bosporus this season; Turkey asked to refrain from Black Sea incursions; both sides aim to keep Sevastopol and coastal zones stable. Austria — Tentative non-aggression understanding: Russia will not immediately rush Galicia if Austria refrains from moves into Ukraine/Poland/Warsaw; both open to later coordination against Turkey or other threats. Public/global messages from France/England/Turkey indicate they prefer restrained openings and will react to German moves; England warned against German quick strikes through Belgium. No binding commitments from Germany, France, Italy recorded.
Intent: S1901M orders: maintain a restrained, multi-front posture. Intended moves to issue: F STP/SC holds (support Black Sea neutrality and signal non-aggression to Turkey); F SEV holds (do not enter ANK/BLA); A MOS hold or move flexible to support Warsaw if needed (but avoid overt aggression this phase); A WAR hold or move to GAL only if Austria explicitly demilitarizes Galicia — otherwise do not advance into Galicia/Ukraine. I intend to keep these promises: I will not cross into ANK/CON/SMY or push into Galicia this turn unless provoked or Austria violates the understanding. I will communicate moves to England and Turkey to preserve trust and request confirmation from Austria on Galicia demilitarization before any advance.

[S1901M] No one moved against our Black Sea promises — Turkey held ANK/CON/SMY and we held SEV/STP. My DMZ offer was effectively matched by Turkey’s restraint; that tacit pact stands. Austria, however, quietly reinforced Galicia from BUD→GAL and VIE supported it: they occupied the space I had promised not to rush into, though I had not committed to demilitarize first. England and France stayed conservative in the west, Germany made a routine Baltic probe but no threat to our cores. No Russian breaches this turn — I honored my restraint to preserve options.

Impact: our southern front remains stable; Austria’s occupation of GAL raises the cost of any Polish/Ukraine advance and signals early Austrian assertiveness. Trust ratings: Turkey reliable; Austria cautious-aggressive; England/France/Germany status quo. Lesson: require explicit demilitarization confirmations before shifting Warsaw or Moskow forces south; keep fleets visibly neutral to maintain Turkish détente while probing diplomatic guarantees from Vienna.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I prioritized secure coasts and the Straits and sought DMZs in the Black Sea and the northern Balkans. Russia agreed to avoid pushing into ANK/CON/SMY and proposed a southeastern Black Sea DMZ if Turkey refrains from moving fleets through the Bosporus; we have a verbal understanding to keep the southern Black Sea calm this season. Austria was asked to respect BUL and RUM as a neutral buffer; they were receptive to a non‑aggression understanding along the BUL–RUM line to avoid Balkan escalation. England offered to act as a neutral messenger and confirmed no Eastern Mediterranean designs; Italy offered to avoid IJ/AEG pressure if I want a quiet southern phase. In short: tacit DMZ with Russia in the southeastern Black Sea, non‑aggression with Austria across BUL–RUM, and informal non‑aggression/cooperation understandings with England and Italy to keep the central Mediterranean and northern seas stable.
Intent: Hold defensive posture this turn to secure Turkish home centers and the Straits. Orders intent: keep F ANK and A SMY/ A CON oriented to defend coasts/Straits and avoid provocative Black Sea transits. Specifically: A CON hold (defend Constantinople and the approaches), F ANK hold (protect southern Black Sea coast), A SMY hold (protect Smyrna and Aegean approach). I intend to honor my agreements: no BLA or transit through the Bosporus; no aggressive moves into BUL/RUM/SEV. I will communicate these concrete moves to Russia, Austria, England, and Italy and expect reciprocal restraint this season.

[S1901M] I held to my plan: ANK, CON and SMY all stayed put to protect the Straits and coasts and honor the tacit DMZs. Russia likewise honored our understanding—SEV and STP held, no push into ANK/CON/SMY or the southern Black Sea—so our southeastern Black Sea quiet is intact for now. Austria respected BUL–RUM restraint by not pressing beyond GAL/BUD moves; Italy and England made non‑threatening Mediterranean moves. No betrayals this phase and I broke no promises. The stable, cautious season preserves my three SCs and gives time to deepen guarantees with Russia and Austria. Lesson: keep clear, limited commitments and verify them with reciprocal public holds; use next diplomatic window to formalize a southern Black Sea DMZ and a BUL–RUM buffer.

support.order: A VIE S A BUD - GAL

goals: Secure and hold home supply centers LON, EDI, LVP by patrolling Channel and North Sea.
Maintain flexible diplomacy in the west: keep France and Germany from early naval/Belgian clashes through signalling and non‑aggression.
Avoid continental entanglements; act as broker to reduce Balkan/Central European cascades (use warnings about Austrian aggression).
Be prepared to coordinate fleet responses into Channel/North Sea if any power contests Belgian/Dutch centers or threatens English home waters.
